scope: A deployer-side readout for a mid-market company deploying an AI agent (e.g. the reference support-email triage agent), framed so a CTO can act on the basics without a lawyer. Distinguishes what is in force now from what is deferred, and states the Digital Omnibus's exact legal status. This readout is NOT legal advice and does not make a deployer compliant — it identifies which obligations apply and where the stack helps you be ready.

omnibusStatus.name: Digital Omnibus on AI
omnibusStatus.stage: Provisional agreement reached by the European Parliament and Council on 2026-05-07; NOT yet formally adopted and NOT yet published in the Official Journal as of 2026-06-24.
omnibusStatus.legalEffect: None yet. Until formally adopted and published in the Official Journal, its deferrals are proposed, not law — a deployer must plan against the dates currently in force (notably 2026-08-02), treating the deferrals as likely-but-not-guaranteed relief.
ifAdopted: Annex III high-risk obligations deferred from 2026-08-02 to 2026-12-02 (2027 in the original plan note; the provisional agreement sets 2026-12-02 for some duties — see per-obligation notes).
Product-integrated (Annex I) high-risk rules deferred to 2028-08-02.
Art 50(2) synthetic-content marking: generative systems already on the market before 2026-08-02 get a transitional period to 2026-12-02.

penaltyTiers.cap: €35,000,000 or 7% of total worldwide annual turnover, whichever is higher
€15,000,000 or 3% of total worldwide annual turnover, whichever is higher
penaltyTiers.appliesTo: Deployer obligations under Art 26 and other operator obligations, incl. high-risk non-compliance (Art 99(4))

penaltyTiers.cap: €7,500,000 or 1% of total worldwide annual turnover, whichever is higher
penaltyTiers.appliesTo: Supplying incorrect, incomplete or misleading information to authorities (Art 99(5))

penaltyNote: SMEs and start-ups are fined the LOWER of the fixed sum or the percentage. The three tiers must NOT be conflated — only Art 5 prohibited practices carry the €35M/7% cap.

statusLegend.supported: The stack ships a concrete technical capability that materially readies a deployer for this obligation (mechanism named).
statusLegend.partial: The stack contributes inputs, but meeting the obligation is substantially the deployer's process/legal work.
statusLegend.deployer-owned: A legal/process obligation the stack cannot satisfy. Listed so it is never silently assumed; the obligation is the deployer's.

applicabilityLegend.from-2026-08-02: Applies from 2026-08-02 under the current law.
applicabilityLegend.conditional-high-risk: Applies only if the deployed system is high-risk (Annex III); for a limited-risk agent it is good practice, not a legal duty.
applicabilityLegend.provider-only: An obligation on the model/system provider, not the deployer — listed for context; a mid-market deployer relies on its provider here.

obligations.title: Do not deploy prohibited AI practices (manipulation, social scoring, untargeted scraping for facial recognition, etc.).

mechanisms.name: Gateway server-side guardrails (prompt-injection/manipulation denial)
mechanisms.note: Reduce manipulative-prompt risk, but do not legally classify a use case as prohibited — that judgement is the deployer's.

obligations.note: Already in force. The decisive control is choosing a non-prohibited use case; the stack lowers manipulation risk but cannot make a prohibited purpose lawful.

obligations.title: Ensure staff operating/overseeing the AI have sufficient AI literacy.

mechanisms.name: AI Reliability Scorecard + blueprint/assessment docs as literacy material
mechanisms.note: Board-legible, plain-language artifacts your team can learn from; the training program itself is the deployer's.

obligations.note: Already in force. The stack supplies legible material; running literacy training is the deployer's.

obligations.title: Transparency: tell people when they are interacting with an AI system, and disclose AI-generated/manipulated content where required.

mechanisms.name: Structured output + human-in-the-loop draft review
mechanisms.ref: @grade-stack/core (zodValidator); reference-agent (drafts a reply a human sends)
mechanisms.note: The agent's output is structured and human-reviewed, making an AI-generated disclosure straightforward to attach; the disclosure text and policy are the deployer's.

obligations.note: Applies 2026-08-02. A Code of Practice on AI-generated-content transparency was published 2026-06-10; signing it is one route to demonstrate compliance.

obligations.title: High-risk deployer duty: ensure human oversight by competent persons and use the system per the provider's instructions.
obligations.deferralNote: If the Digital Omnibus is adopted, Annex III high-risk duties move to 2026-12-02 — not yet law (see omnibusStatus).

mechanisms.name: Planner/Executor/Validator + human-reviewed drafts + enforced --max-turns
mechanisms.note: Human-in-the-loop by design; the validator and bounded loop give a competent person a reviewable, bounded decision to oversee.

obligations.note: Legally required only if your agent is Annex III high-risk; good practice regardless. The reference support-triage agent is most likely NOT Annex III.

obligations.title: High-risk deployer duty: keep automatically generated logs / records of the system's operation.
obligations.deferralNote: Deferred to 2026-12-02 if the Digital Omnibus is adopted (not yet law).

mechanisms.name: OpenTelemetry tracing (connected plan→tool→validate trace per run)
mechanisms.note: Produces durable, inspectable per-run records — the technical substrate of record-keeping.

obligations.note: Strongest area of stack readiness: if you go high-risk, the logging substrate already exists.

obligations.title: High-risk deployer duty: monitor operation and accuracy/robustness; act on and report serious incidents.
obligations.deferralNote: Deferred to 2026-12-02 if the Digital Omnibus is adopted (not yet law).

mechanisms.name: Eval suite + CI eval-gate vs committed baseline + reliability scorecard
mechanisms.note: Continuous accuracy/robustness measurement against a baseline, with a board-legible readout — the monitoring half of the duty.

obligations.note: Incident reporting to authorities remains a deployer process; the measurement and detection substrate is provided.

obligations.title: Fundamental-rights impact assessment for certain high-risk deployers (e.g. public bodies, essential private services).
obligations.deferralNote: Deferred to 2026-12-02 if the Digital Omnibus is adopted (not yet law).
obligations.note: A legal/process assessment no software stack performs. Listed so it is never silently assumed. The stack's traces and scorecard are inputs a FRIA can cite, but the assessment is the deployer's.

obligations.title: Registration in the EU database and conformity assessment for high-risk systems.
obligations.deferralNote: Deferred to 2026-12-02 if the Digital Omnibus is adopted (not yet law).
obligations.note: Primarily provider-led; deployer registration duties apply to some (e.g. public-authority) deployers. A legal/administrative process the stack cannot perform.

obligations.title: Mark synthetic (AI-generated) content in a machine-readable way.
obligations.deferralNote: If the Digital Omnibus is adopted, generative systems already on the market before 2026-08-02 get a transitional period to 2026-12-02 (not yet law).

mechanisms.name: Structured, machine-readable output
mechanisms.note: As a deployer you rely on your model provider here; if you act as a provider, structured output eases machine-readable marking.

obligations.note: Provider-side obligation; a mid-market deployer relies on its model provider. Listed for context, not scored against deployer readiness.

obligations.title: General-purpose AI model provider obligations (technical documentation, copyright policy, training-data summary).
obligations.note: On the GPAI model provider (e.g. the foundation-model vendor behind your agent), not on you as a deployer. The provider abstraction lets you choose a compliant provider, but the obligation is theirs.
